questions.question: Why were many girls not sent to school in the past?
questions.correct_answer: Because society believed girls only needed to do household work
questions.explanation: Traditional beliefs often prevented girls from receiving formal education.

questions.question: Who was Savitribai Phule?
questions.correct_answer: A social reformer who worked to promote education for girls
questions.explanation: She opened one of the first schools for girls in India along with Jyotirao Phule.

questions.question: Who was Rashsundari Devi?
questions.correct_answer: A woman who secretly learned to read and wrote the autobiography 'Amar Jiban'
questions.explanation: Her story shows the struggles women faced to gain education.

questions.question: What is gender inequality?
questions.correct_answer: Unequal treatment or opportunities for men and women
questions.explanation: In many societies women were given fewer rights and opportunities than men.

questions.question: Why is education important for women?
questions.correct_answer: It gives them knowledge, confidence, and better opportunities
questions.explanation: Education helps women participate actively in society and make informed decisions.

questions.question: What are gender stereotypes?
questions.correct_answer: Fixed ideas about how men and women should behave
questions.explanation: These beliefs often limit the opportunities available to girls and women.

questions.question: How do self-help groups support women?
questions.correct_answer: They help women work together, save money, and start small businesses
questions.explanation: Self-help groups promote financial independence and empowerment.

questions.question: Name one field where women have made significant achievements.
questions.explanation: Women today work successfully in many professions.

questions.question: What does women's empowerment mean?
questions.correct_answer: Giving women equal rights, opportunities, and decision-making power
questions.explanation: Empowerment allows women to participate equally in society.

questions.question: How does educating girls benefit society?
questions.correct_answer: It improves family well-being, economic growth, and social development
questions.explanation: Educated women contribute to healthier and more prosperous communities.

notes.title: Girls' Education
notes.content: Education helps girls gain independence, knowledge, and opportunities.

notes.title: Women Reformers
notes.content: Reformers like Savitribai Phule worked to improve access to education for girls.

notes.title: Gender Equality
notes.content: Ensuring equal rights and opportunities for both men and women.

notes.title: Women's Empowerment
notes.content: Education and financial independence help women make decisions about their lives.

notes.title: Breaking Stereotypes
notes.content: Women entering different professions challenge traditional gender roles.

facts.fact: Savitribai Phule is considered one of the pioneers of girls' education in India.
Rashsundari Devi wrote one of the earliest autobiographies by an Indian woman.
Education helps reduce gender inequality in society.
Women today contribute significantly to economic and social development.
